mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce borrowing costs and discount rates, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and business demand strength, driving sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with a t-stat > 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production below -0.5% signals immediate supply-side contraction, prompting short-term market sell-offs.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when industrial production contracts sharply.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate indicate a strong economy absorbing tighter policy, supporting positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative average 20-day return or a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand, which translates into stronger corporate earnings and positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation (p > 0.05) with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation readings exceeding 2.5% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a t-stat > 1.64 or shows negative predictive power in walk-forward validation.
